skill.name: specdd-risk
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to classify risk before SpecDD work starts.
skill.summary: Use when Agent needs to classify risk before SpecDD work starts.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code around SpecDD file paths. The weak cryptography findings are also false positives caused by ordinary text, including frontmatter and the word destructive. No malicious intent was found, but the skill relies on project-local SpecDD files as instructions, which creates a low trust-boundary risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Static findings at the listed locations are Markdown inline code for SpecDD paths or references, not Ruby or shell execution. No command runner, script, or executable payload is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows Markdown inline code around file paths and terms. There is no syntax that invokes Ruby, a shell, or any external command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static findings at the listed locations match ordinary prose, not cryptographic configuration. The skill does not mention a weak algorithm or implement cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain the frontmatter description and the word destructive. I did not find evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptography usage.

low_findings.title: Project-Local Instruction Trust Boundary
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read and apply local SpecDD bootstrap and spec files. This is expected behavior, but users should treat those files as project-scoped instructions and preserve higher-priority safety rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly directs reading and applying local project documents. This is a legitimate workflow, but it creates a limited trust-boundary concern.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need risk calls before planning, editing, or reviewing work. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through bootstrap-aware risk classification.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a project has the required SpecDD bootstrap file.
Guides agents to resolve bootstrap files in the correct order.
Directs agents to identify the effective spec chain for a target.
Classifies planned work as low, medium, or high risk.
Highlights risk factors such as public APIs, migrations, secrets, and destructive operations.
Requires a smallest safe next step and verification plan.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already use SpecDD.
Does not create, modify, or adopt SpecDD files by itself.
Does not replace human judgment for security-sensitive changes.
Depends on accurate local bootstrap and spec files.

use_cases.title: Pre-Change Risk Review
use_cases.description: Classify the risk of a requested change before planning or editing starts.

use_cases.title: Spec Authority Check
use_cases.description: Confirm which local specs grant write authority for a target path.

use_cases.title: Release-Sensitive Decision Support
use_cases.description: Identify higher-risk changes that touch APIs, data, auth, migrations, or releases.

prompt_templates.title: Classify a Simple Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner risk call for one requested edit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-risk to classify this requested change before editing. Identify the governing SpecDD files, risk level, reasons, smallest safe next step, and verification needed.

prompt_templates.title: Check Write Authority
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm whether a target path can be edited.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-risk to resolve the bootstrap chain and effective spec chain for this target. Tell me whether write authority is clear, missing, or conflicting.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Cross-Boundary Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a change that may cross ownership or contract boundaries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-risk to assess this change across affected files and specs. Highlight public contracts, data shape, ownership boundaries, test gaps, and release concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a High-Risk Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before work involving sensitive systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-risk to evaluate this implementation plan before execution. Focus on auth, secrets, migrations, irreversible operations, external systems, and verification gates.

output_examples.input: Classify risk before changing a README section in a SpecDD project.
output_examples.output: Risk is low because the change is documentation-only and does not affect contracts, data, auth, or releases. Verify the governing spec allows the edit and review the rendered text.

output_examples.input: Classify risk before changing a database migration and API response shape.
output_examples.output: Risk is high because the change affects persistent data and a public contract. Confirm write authority, migration rollback expectations, compatibility tests, and release coordination.

output_examples.input: Check whether a local bootstrap file changes project rules.
output_examples.output: Review the bootstrap chain in order and compare local rules against inherited constraints. Local rules may specialize behavior but must not silently weaken project contracts.

best_practices: Read only the bootstrap and spec files needed for the current decision.
State concrete risk reasons tied to files, contracts, data, or verification gaps.
End every risk call with the smallest safe next step.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in a project without an existing SpecDD bootstrap file.
Inferring write authority from nearby filenames or conventions.
Treating a low risk call as permission to skip verification.

faq.question: Does this skill edit files?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to assess risk and avoid edits unless explicitly asked.

faq.question: Can it be used outside SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. It requires an existing root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: What risk levels does it use?
faq.answer: It classifies work as low, medium, or high risk with concrete reasons.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The file uses Markdown path references, not executable commands.

faq.question: What files does it ask the agent to read?
faq.answer: It asks for relevant SpecDD bootstrap files and governing specs needed for the current decision.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Engineers, reviewers, and release owners who need risk classification before SpecDD work.
